text: This is the testing process for this reactor.

text: First, I will describe the gas feed conditions.

text: The anode feed gas is hydrogen with a flow rate of 20 fcl.

text: The cathode feed gas is carbon monoxide with a flow rate of 10 fcl.

text: The working electrode is connected to the cathode.

text: The counter electrode is connected to the anode.

text: The reference electrode is placed in the middle electrolyte chamber.

text: The reference electrode is a mercury oxide electrode.

text: During the test, the middle chamber is filled with a circulating 1-molecule H liquid electrolyte.

text: The middle chamber thickness is 3 millimeter.

text: The circulating 1-molecule H provides a continuous ionic PSV between the cathode side am and anode side am.

text: The electrolyte flow rate is 30 fcl.

text: The purpose of reducing the chamber thickness is to shorten the ion transparent distance, reduce the ionic resistance and improve the overall cell and reduce the cell voltage.

text: At the same time, the reference electrode allows to monitor the electrode potentials microdifferentially and on-off operation.

text: For this test, we will first perform a constant current operation to monitor the cell voltage stability during electrolysis.

text: After the test, we will perform the electrochemical impedance spectropase or PEIS to evaluate the resistance of the electrode after generation.
